rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL E. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 04, 2020 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT OFFICE OF COMMUNITY INVESTMENT AND INFRASTRUCTURE's DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Cross-Defendant OCII's Demurrer to Second Amended Cross-Complaint is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. As alleged the fifth and the sixth causes of action are barred by the Statute of Limitations and by failure to comply with the Government Claims Act. Cross-Complainant does not suggest any amendments that could cure these deficiencies. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL E. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 04, 2020 line 1. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL JOHNSON AND URBAN CORE DEVELOPMENT, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT . Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment. is GRANTED. Moving parties shifted their burden re: liability based on alter ego. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare an order in accordance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MICHAEL E. JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 04, 2020 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended CROSS COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Cross-Defendant CCSF's Demurrer to Second Amended Cross-Complaint. is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The third and fourth causes of action as alleged are subject to the Government Claims act and do not fall under the exception outlined in Krainock v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 216 Cal.App.3d 1473. Cross-Complainant does not suggest any amendments that would satisfy the second and the third requirements under Krainock. Furthermore, as alleged, the third cause of action does not contain any facts showing that the City failed to negotiate in good faith. The liability is based on City's failure to accept a specific term. (SAXC 41). Finally, the fourth cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations. The cause of action was complete at the time of OCII's alleged breach as stated in the SAXC at paragraph 49. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: XIAOMEI FENG ET AL VS. JIAWEI JIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action For Plaintiffs Failure To Amend Complaint After Demurrer Was Sustained And Request For Entry Of Judgment Of Dismissal; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Arasto Farsad In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Aug. 04, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT JIAWEI JIAN'S Motion To Dismiss Action For Plaintiffs Failure To Amend Complaint After Demurrer Was Sustained And Request For Entry Of Judgment Of Dismissal Hearing Required for Plaintiff's Counsel to clearly state whether she is making a request for relief under CCP 473(b). = (501/CFH)
